item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely loved this! The way Peaches spoke, the white text at the end, but my favorite part was test #101. I love that when enraged, THAT is what is going through its head! Gave me a good little chuckle in what honestly turned out to be a somewhat sad ending of a great scip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for pointing it out! I would have missed it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That white text at the end gave me shivers, and that was a perfect way to end it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel there are too many moving parts to this, most of the test logs are fun but a distraction and Peaches’ interview shifts the tone to the ominous, but isn’t really paid off

I do think that the log from the previous owner is insanely well written and ends on such a somber note that I’m upvoting for the feels… but I feel the path to get there is muddied by what we see before and ultimately distracts from the core message

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image is taken by the author and thus considered CC BY-SA 3.0 compliant.

item.title: Easy +1
item.content:encoded:
I have been with this article since it’s early days and I must say that my love for it has not changed - it’s great. The crosstesting and ending are really well done (poor Judy :c). The only problem I have is the testing with Peaches, as it sort of adds nothing to the article, but it’s a very short part of the whole thing so it isn’t a large problem - but maybe giving it a finishing touch or two would maybe make it a bit better.
It’s such a shame it only has +11 at the time of me writing this, as it deserves so much more attention.
Great work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're not the first one to go "Normally I hate cross-testing, but-", and I'm glad that's how it is lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going to roll my eyes when 096 came up, but honestly, it's one of the more tasteful uses of a series 1 cross-link I've seen. Overall, I like it, and this needs more people reading it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Congratulations on posting your SCP! It was a fun experience to see how you developed your SCP; however, Sebarus has a point in the testing log. But I'm sure you'll improve as time advances.

P.D: If your box plays the song, does that mean it can read my pet's thoughts?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While normally test logs and cross-references to Series 1 skips make me roll my eyes, I actually found this one somewhat intriguing.

I was somewhat dissapointed when the plot thread in the interview with Peaches didn't really go anywhere. We're given this hint to some mysterious, malevolent nature behind this anomaly, but then it's kinda just left hanging.

The following plotline with Jude and Sparky, however, was enough to turn this around for me. It's somewhat simplistic and predictable, but it was effective enough to make me feel bad for the characters, and usually that's enough to get an upvote out of me.

item.title: oh lawd my first skip
item.content:encoded:
Huge thank you to Comrade Waldo, Ralliston, Opal, Magic8Ball04, and a few others for helping me out. Ya'll pushed me to develop the idea waaaay better
Image is taken by me. I own that music box! It's sitting on a shelf in my room, and yes, it actually does play Zip-a-Dee-Doo-Dah.
